title: Docs truth cleanup: Codex DOES have hooks + trust-verify procedure
action: Per D3 (advisor-repaired): replace the stale claims at INSTALL.md:120 ('Codex has no lifecycle hooks'), README.md:434 ('Codex has no hooks'), and docs/06-runtime-integration.md:52 with accurate prose: Codex loads project hooks from .codex/hooks.json (7 events shipped; diagnosed working on Codex CLI 0.144.1 per codex-runtime-parity reports). Add to INSTALL.md a Codex verify procedure with EVENT-SPECIFIC evidence (advisor finding 6): (1) project .codex/ trusted; (2) hook trust review — present the /hooks step CONDITIONALLY ('where the installed Codex version exposes /hooks, review and trust the bee hooks; trust semantics are being confirmed by the capability spike') — do NOT assert unverified /hooks semantics as fact; (3) observed check: in a working Codex session, PostToolUse successes are recorded by bee-tools-logger in .bee/logs/tools.jsonl (the general success log), while .bee/logs/hooks.jsonl records only crashes and narrow subagent lifecycle cases — NEVER claim a healthy session necessarily adds a hooks.jsonl row. Document the three-state model verbatim: hooks_file_present ≠ hooks_discovered ≠ hooks_trusted_and_observed. VERIFY GUARD: never quote the banned phrases ('Codex has no hooks', 'Codex has no lifecycle hooks') verbatim anywhere in these three files; paraphrase ('the earlier claim that Codex lacked hook support'). Keep surrounding prose voice; do not rewrite unrelated sections. 06-runtime-integration.md: rewrite the Codex row to name the real hook events and drop the 'accepted gaps ... if Codex grows hook support' framing where obsolete.

truths: grep for the two banned phrases over the three files returns 0
INSTALL.md contains the three-state model and a conditional hook-trust step for Codex
INSTALL.md cites .bee/logs/tools.jsonl for PostToolUse success evidence (not hooks.jsonl)

artifacts.substantive: Codex verify procedure: trust, conditional /hooks, event-specific observed evidence

prohibitions: No changes to Claude-side install instructions
No removal of the AGENTS.md-block bootstrap documentation
No unconditional /hooks semantics asserted before the spike confirms them

verify: bash -c '! grep -n "Codex has no hooks\|Codex has no lifecycle hooks" INSTALL.md README.md docs/06-runtime-integration.md && grep -qi "hooks_trusted_and_observed" INSTALL.md && grep -q "tools.jsonl" INSTALL.md && echo DOCS-CLEAN'

trace.outcome: Replaced the three stale 'Codex has no (lifecycle) hooks' claims (INSTALL.md:120, README.md:434, docs/06-runtime-integration.md:52) with paraphrased truth naming the 7 shipped .codex/hooks.json events, and added a three-state Codex hook verify procedure (trust / conditional /hooks review / event-specific observed evidence via .bee/logs/tools.jsonl) to INSTALL.md sec.4. Also updated the obsolete 'accepted gaps ... if Codex grows hook support' framing in docs/06-runtime-integration.md to note the gaps are now mechanism-present, capability-spike-pending rather than absent.
trace.verify_command: bash -c "! grep -n \"Codex has no hooks\\|Codex has no lifecycle hooks\" INSTALL.md README.md docs/06-runtime-integration.md && grep -qi \"hooks_trusted_and_observed\" INSTALL.md && grep -q \"tools.jsonl\" INSTALL.md && echo DOCS-CLEAN"
